observe: This exact shape repeats everywhere — someone filling a buffer, someone else draining it. You've seen this before, even if you don't remember it.
fault: The buffer overflowed. Nobody checked before writing, and now it's holding more than it can.
repair: Balance it. Producer, consumer, one full cycle at a time.
escape: Keep the cycle balanced — don't let it overflow again.

observe: Classic synchronization problems include producer-consumer, readers-writers, and dining philosophers.
fault: The shared buffer has exceeded its capacity.
repair: Balance production and consumption to complete a full cycle.
escape: Complete the required number of cycles before time expires.

hints: Watch the buffer level — it should never go above capacity or below zero.
